label: The P0 implementation set is metadata and cli-opencode reference work: model profile, per-model budget row, context-budget mirror, and cli_reference MiniMax variant/routing text.
The P1 implementation set is routing and sentinel discoverability: cli-opencode SKILL.md, sk-prompt budget-awareness card, sk-prompt-small-model SKILL.md, and pattern-index rows.
The MiniMax variant ablation manual test is useful but optional because it requires live MINIMAX_API_KEY and should not block schema-compatible documentation updates.
Runtime-deferred residuals are limited to live provider facts: exact slug casing, variant acceptance/effect, highspeed slug, latency, rate-limit errors, tool event shape, and current pricing.
Follow-on implementation must not add MiniMax-specific runtime branches; permissions, fallback, and output verification remain provider-neutral or registry-driven.
Follow-on implementation must refine the existing Phase 001 MiniMax provider/profile path, not duplicate it.
MiniMax remains explicit-route and fail-fast; no silent fallback to or from minimax-api.
Current model-profiles.json already contains exactly one minimax-2.7 object with minimax-api quota pool and fallback_target null; update confidence is high.
Current cli-opencode context-budget mirror omits MiniMax and has no active-budget column; patch can update the table without changing runtime logic.
Current sk-prompt-small-model pattern index is link-only; MiniMax rows should stay link-only and point at executor-owned files.
Exact accepted MiniMax slug/casing requires MINIMAX_API_KEY plus opencode models minimax.
Real --variant behavior requires paired no-variant vs --variant high opencode run ablation against minimax/minimax-2.7.
Observed latency, timeout envelope, rate-limit error shapes, tool-call JSON event shape, and current pricing require live MiniMax provider access.
